signals.text: Brand confirmed cancellation of long-term commitment on Mar 31 retention call with Maddie
Brand Churn Notice filed by Pulkit on Mar 31 to churns@fermatcommerce.com
Platform UI/UX did not match sales demo expectations per Maddie; desktop optimization described as severely lacking
Opt-out date is Apr 22 -- one week from today. No follow-up meeting notes after Mar 31 in Granola
Split testing never beat existing landing pages per brand feedback on $10k ad spend
Month-to-month $2,000 option offered (down from $3,000) but no confirmed acceptance found in data
Invoice payment issues: Jan and Feb invoices paid late on Mar 27; March invoice still outstanding as of Apr 1

recent.title: Mother Root <> FERMAT | Weekly Check In
recent.summary: Customer check-in covering funnel builder topics. Short 11-minute call. Regular weekly cadence with Maddie and Thomas from Mother Root.

recent.title: Mother Root <> FERMAT | Weekly Check In
recent.summary: Customer check-in with DPP and Funnel Builder discussion. Competitive mentions and objections raised. Longer 34-minute call suggesting substantive issues discussed.

recent.title: Mother Root <> FERMAT | Weekly Check In
recent.summary: Customer check-in with Funnel Builder, DPP discussion. Competitive mentions, objections raised, and technical/scoping topics covered. 30-minute call with multiple product areas discussed.

calls.analysis: Mother Root had regular weekly check-ins through Jan-Feb 2026 managed by Talia Cohen. However, Glyphic shows the last recorded call was Feb 13, and weekly cadence stopped afterward. Granola shows a critical retention call on Mar 31 where Maddie confirmed cancellation. The brand raised issues about platform UI/UX not matching sales demo expectations, poor desktop optimization, and split testing that never beat existing landing pages. A $2,000/mo month-to-month option was offered but acceptance is unconfirmed.

slack.analysis: No Slack MCP available for this refresh.

messages.subject: Re: Brand Churn Notice: MOTHER ROOT
messages.snippet: Noted on the contracts being the most reliable data source. I will reach out to the MOTHER ROOT team to confirm the March invoice status.

messages.subject: Re: Brand Churn Notice: MOTHER ROOT
messages.snippet: Contracts are the mostly reliable data sources. Also can you confirm for the march invoice?

messages.subject: Re: Brand Churn Notice: MOTHER ROOT
messages.snippet: Understood on the $3000 MRR discrepancy. I saw this number on SFDC and have been following this as the source for all my accounts.

messages.subject: Re: Brand Churn Notice: MOTHER ROOT
messages.snippet: Mother Root has paid us on 3/27/26 for invoices INV-0003063 ($1500 due 1/31) and INV-0003155 ($1500 due 2/28). This leaves only one invoice outstanding INV-0003298.

messages.subject: Re: Brand Churn Notice: MOTHER ROOT
messages.snippet: The Mother Root team hasn't paid any of their invoices yet. Can you please check up with them before we churn them out.

messages.subject: Brand Churn Notice: MOTHER ROOT
messages.snippet: Customer Name: MOTHER ROOT. Contact: Maddie Carey (Growth Marketing Manager), Thomas Beverley (Co-Founder).

messages.subject: MOTHER ROOT x FERMAT || Next Steps
messages.snippet: Hi Maddie, Really appreciate you taking the time to chat today and for being so open and honest about your experience. Just to confirm what we discussed... [DRAFT]

email.analysis: All email activity centers around the churn process initiated on Mar 31. Pulkit filed the Brand Churn Notice, followed by internal discussion with Ashutosh and Alec about invoice status and MRR discrepancy ($3000 vs contract amount). Alec confirmed Jan and Feb invoices were paid late on Mar 27, with March invoice outstanding. A draft follow-up email to Maddie was prepared but it is unclear if it was sent. No email activity found after Apr 1, suggesting the account has gone quiet.
